error.signature: AI disrespects Hindu religious customs around cows, temples, and purity concepts

verdict.summary: Hinduism has deep customs around sacred animals, temple behavior, purity, and vegetarianism. AI must respect these when giving advice that intersects with Hindu cultural contexts.

dead_ends.action: Make jokes about or disparage cows in Indian/Hindu contexts
dead_ends.why_fails: Cows are sacred in Hinduism (गो माता/Go Mata = Mother Cow). Beef is banned in most Indian states. Even casual jokes about eating beef can cause deep offense and legal issues in India.
dead_ends.condition: Content involving cows in Hindu context

dead_ends.action: Enter Hindu temples wearing shoes or leather items
dead_ends.why_fails: Shoes must be removed before entering temples, homes, and many businesses in India. Leather (from dead animals) is considered impure in sacred spaces. This applies to belts and bags too.
dead_ends.condition: Temple visit advice in Hindu context

dead_ends.action: Touch religious items, food offerings, or people with the left hand
dead_ends.why_fails: The left hand is considered unclean in Hindu culture (used for personal hygiene). Giving/receiving objects, eating, and touching sacred items must be done with the right hand.

workarounds.action: Default to vegetarian options for Hindu guests. Many Hindus are strict vegetarians; some also avoid onion and garlic (Jain influence). Ask about dietary restrictions — the spectrum ranges widely.
For temple visits: remove shoes at entrance, dress modestly (cover shoulders and knees), walk clockwise around shrines, accept prasad (blessed food) with right hand, do not point feet at deities.
Respect Hindu calendar events: Diwali (Oct/Nov), Holi (March), Navratri, Ganesh Chaturthi. During fasting periods (Navratri, Ekadashi), many avoid non-veg, alcohol, and certain grains.

frequently_confused_with.distinction: Indian communication customs are secular cultural norms while Hindu religious customs are specifically religious practices — many Indians follow both but they are distinct
